provenance.pin_detail: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

privilege_notes: runs fetched package code at launch

findings.title: Installed on-the-fly via a package runner
findings.detail: Server 'unreal-engine' is fetched and executed at launch via 'npx' with auto-accept flag(s) -y; package spec: unreal-engine-mcp-server.
findings.recommendation: Install the server as a pinned dependency and invoke the local binary, or vendor it. If a runner is unavoidable, pin an exact version (see unpinned_source).

findings.title: Package source is unpinned or mutable
findings.detail: Server 'unreal-engine' package spec 'unreal-engine-mcp-server' is unpinned (pin strength: none): no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now).
findings.recommendation: Pin an exact version (pkg@1.2.3) or a git commit SHA. Avoid @latest and branch refs.

evidence.reason: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

tools.name: manage_sessions
tools.description:
Configure local multiplayer: split-screen layouts, LAN hosting/joining, voice chat channels, and push-to-talk.

Required parameters by action:
- configure_local_session_settings: At least one setting param required
- configure_session_interface: interfaceType
- configure_split_screen: enabled or splitScreenType
- set_split_screen_type: splitScreenType
- add_local_player: controllerId (requires PIE)
- remove_local_player: playerIndex (requires PIE)
- configure_lan_play: enabled, serverPort, or serverPassword
- host_lan_server: mapName (requires PIE)
- join_lan_server: serverAddress (requires PIE)
- enable_voice_chat: voiceEnabled
- configure_voice_settings: voiceSettings
- set_voice_channel: channelName
- mute_player: playerName or targetPlayerId
- set_voice_attenuation: attenuationRadius
- configure_push_to_talk: pushToTalkEnabled
- get_sessions_info: No additional params required

candidate_capabilities.title: Outbound network / data egress

evidence.snippet: D for host_lan_server. Travel URL options string. Enable/disabl

candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that makes outbound calls can exfiltrate anything it can read (including the server's own credentials) to an attacker-chosen host.
candidate_capabilities.validation_reason: "Travel URL" is for game level transitions within the engine, not external network requests.

candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that surfaces secrets pairs dangerously with any network_egress tool on the same server — read-then-send is a complete exfil chain.

data_profile.note: Rating reflects high-confidence matches; communications_content, credentials_secrets matched only in prose (medium confidence) and would raise the rating if confirmed in review.

toxic_combinations.title: Read-then-send exfiltration chain
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can both access secrets/credentials and make outbound network calls — a path to exfiltrate them to an attacker-chosen host.

toxic_combinations.title: Code execution alongside secret access
toxic_combinations.detail: Arbitrary execution combined with credential access means a single tool call can read and abuse every secret the server holds.

toxic_combinations.title: File/message read paired with egress
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can read file/message contents and make outbound network calls — a read-then-send exfiltration path. Severity is MEDIUM because the read appears scoped; confirm what the read tool can reach and where egress can target.

"Directory" is a file path for game framework assets, not contacts.
"team" is a game match team for competitive gameplay, not organizational structure.
"Travel URL" is for game level transitions within the engine, not external network requests.
"label" is a UI display label for data layers, not project task/issue tracking.
"endPoint" is a 3D coordinate for pathfinding destination, not a network endpoint/URL.
